examples.title: Basic Class with Constructors and Destructors
examples.description: Demonstrates fundamental class structure with public members, constructors (default, parameterized), and destructor in C++
examples.explanation:
1. Class 'Student' is defined with public access specifier
2. Default constructor initializes with default values
3. Parameterized constructor accepts arguments to initialize data members
4. display() is a member function that prints student information
5. Destructor is automatically called when object goes out of scope
6. In main(), two objects are created demonstrating both constructors

examples.title: Access Modifiers and Encapsulation
examples.description: Demonstrates private/public access modifiers, data hiding, and getter/setter methods for encapsulation
examples.explanation:
1. Private members (accountHolder, accountNumber, balance) are hidden from direct access
2. Public members (constructors, getters, setters, operations) provide controlled access
3. const member functions (getBalance, display) promise not to modify object state
4. Getter methods provide read-only access to private data
5. Setter methods validate before modifying data (encapsulation)
6. This demonstrates data hiding and encapsulation principles

examples.title: Inheritance, Polymorphism, and Virtual Functions
examples.description: Demonstrates advanced OOP concepts including single inheritance, protected access modifier, virtual functions, and runtime polymorphism
examples.explanation:
1. Base class 'Person' has protected members (accessible to derived classes)
2. virtual keyword enables runtime polymorphism - correct method called based on actual object type
3. override keyword (C++11) ensures method correctly overrides base class virtual function
4. Pure virtual function (= 0) makes class abstract - cannot instantiate Person directly
5. Single inheritance: Student and Teacher inherit from Person
6. Base class pointer (Person*) can point to derived class objects
7. Virtual destructor ensures proper cleanup of derived class objects
8. demonstrateWork() shows runtime polymorphism - same interface, different behavior
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for each method call, O(n) if calling n times
